fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 71 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(active) == limit` the gate incorrectly allows the request instead of denying it.

constraints.intent: When active count equals limit the condition must evaluate to True (deny), but with > it evaluates to False (allow), violating the boundary case.
After the else branch (gate allows), active count must be strictly less than limit; if equal to limit we should not reach here.

spec.expr: not (len(active__1) >= limit) or len(active__1) > limit or len(active__1) == limit

constraints.intent: Before using active in the comparison, verify that the boundary case (len==limit) is handled as a denial condition.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True) or len(active__1) < limit

constraints.intent: The gate should only return allowed=True when active count is strictly less than limit, not equal to it.
